title: Add Server-Sent Events (SSE) stream as alternative to fire-and-forget HTTP POST
source: SSE (EventSource API) best practices for real-time web dashboards — persistent connection reduces latency and eliminates lost events
description: The current fire-and-forget HTTP POST model in roro_events.py means events can be silently lost if Rick Terminal is temporarily unreachable (circuit breaker opens, events during that window are dropped forever). SSE provides a persistent connection where the server (Python side) pushes events to the client (Rick Terminal), with automatic reconnection and last-event-id replay. This is simpler than WebSocket for unidirectional event streams.
proposed_changes: Add an optional SSE server mode to roro_events.py: (1) Create a lightweight SSE server using Python's built-in http.server on a configurable port (default 3069). (2) Buffer last 50 events in a ring buffer so reconnecting clients can replay missed events via Last-Event-ID header. (3) Keep existing HTTP POST as primary mode — SSE is opt-in via config flag `roro.sse_enabled: true`. (4) Rick Terminal can then use EventSource/URLSession streaming to receive events with zero polling and guaranteed delivery. Add config key `sse_port` to .cto/config.json.
